legalSystem.zh: 法律体系继承西班牙殖民法、加的斯宪政传统、独立后宪法与特别法令并行的混合格局。普通民事与刑事司法之外，军人和教士仍享有部分特权法庭。法律条文较多，但执行因地区差异、财政不足与政治动荡而不均。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was a hybrid of inherited Spanish colonial law, Cádiz constitutional traditions, post-independence constitutions, and special decrees. Alongside ordinary civil and criminal justice, military officers and clergy still retained elements of privileged jurisdiction. Legal norms were extensive, but enforcement was uneven because of regional variation, fiscal weakness, and political instability.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入主要依赖海关关税、消费税、烟草等专卖收入、印花税、盐税及临时强制借款。土地直接税征收能力有限，地方抗税与走私普遍。国家因此高度依赖港口贸易税，尤其是韦拉克鲁斯等口岸。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended primarily on customs duties, excise taxes, monopoly revenues such as tobacco, stamp taxes, salt duties, and emergency forced loans. Capacity to levy direct land taxation was limited, while tax resistance and smuggling were common. The state therefore relied heavily on port duties, especially from outlets such as Veracruz.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为压倒性主导宗教，是公共生活、教育、节庆与身份认同的核心。民间信仰中融合了西班牙天主教传统与印第安地方宗教实践，圣母崇拜尤其重要。
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the overwhelmingly dominant religion and stood at the center of public life, education, festivals, and identity. Popular religion blended Spanish Catholic traditions with indigenous local practices, with Marian devotion especially prominent.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想由保守主义、共和主义、联邦主义、中央集权主义与自由主义早期思潮并存。精英教育仍深受经院哲学和天主教道德观影响，同时也逐渐吸收启蒙思想、宪政主义与民族主权观念。
philosophy.en: Political thought combined conservatism, republicanism, federalism, centralism, and early liberal currents. Elite education still reflected scholastic philosophy and Catholic moral teaching, while increasingly absorbing Enlightenment ideas, constitutionalism, and concepts of national sovereignty.

writingSystem.zh: 主要文字系统为拉丁字母书写的西班牙语；部分原住民社区继续使用纳瓦特尔语、玛雅诸语、萨波特克语、米斯特克语等本地语言的口传传统，少量文书也可见拉丁字母拼写的土著语言文本。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the Latin alphabet used for Spanish. Some indigenous communities continued oral traditions in Nahuatl, Mayan languages, Zapotec, Mixtec, and others, and a limited body of texts in native languages written in Latin script also existed.

culturalAchievements.zh: 独立后墨西哥逐渐形成民族国家文化象征，涵盖爱国历史叙事、天主教节庆、军事纪念、版画印刷、城市建筑与音乐传统。首都与普埃布拉、瓜达拉哈拉等城市保留重要的殖民时期建筑、宗教艺术与学术机构。报刊和政治小册子在公共舆论中日益重要。
culturalAchievements.en: Post-independence Mexico was gradually shaping the symbols of a national culture, including patriotic historical narratives, Catholic festivals, military commemorations, prints and pamphlets, urban architecture, and musical traditions. Mexico City, Puebla, Guadalajara, and other cities preserved major colonial-era architecture, religious art, and learned institutions. Newspapers and political pamphlets were becoming increasingly important in public debate.

languageFamily.zh: 官方与统治语言属印欧语系罗曼语族的西班牙语；境内另有乌托-阿兹特克语系、玛雅语系、奥托-曼格语系等众多原住民语言
languageFamily.en: The official and ruling language was Spanish, a Romance language of the Indo-European family; the country also contained many indigenous languages from the Uto-Aztecan, Mayan, Oto-Manguean, and other families

gdpDescription.zh: 1840年的墨西哥经济规模在拉丁美洲仍属重要，但较殖民晚期银矿繁荣时期明显衰弱。独立战争造成矿业、交通与财政体系受损，国内市场破碎，地方关卡与动乱抬高交易成本。尽管如此，白银、农业、畜牧业和港口贸易仍支撑着国家经济基础。
gdpDescription.en: In 1840 Mexico still possessed one of the more significant economies in Latin America, but it was notably weaker than during the late colonial silver boom. The independence struggle had damaged mining, transport, and fiscal institutions, while fragmented internal markets, regional barriers, and unrest raised transaction costs. Even so, silver, agriculture, ranching, and port commerce still underpinned the national economy.

mainIndustries.zh: 白银采矿、金矿和铜矿开采、庄园农业、玉米与豆类生产、小麦种植、甘蔗与酒类、畜牧业、皮革、纺织手工业、烟草加工、染料与国内贸易
mainIndustries.en: Silver mining, smaller-scale gold and copper extraction, hacienda agriculture, maize and bean production, wheat cultivation, sugar and spirits, ranching, leatherwork, textile handicrafts, tobacco processing, dyestuffs, and internal trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以白银、胭脂虫染料、皮革、糖、香草、染木和少量农牧产品为主；进口以英国和美国纺织品、金属制品、工具、枪械、奢侈品、纸张和部分机械为主
tradeGoods.en: Exports centered on silver, cochineal dye, hides, sugar, vanilla, dyewoods, and smaller volumes of agricultural and pastoral products; imports included British and American textiles, metal goods, tools, firearms, luxury items, paper, and some machinery

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分布极不平等。少数地主、矿主、商人与教会机构拥有大量土地、房产和贵金属资产；多数农村家庭依赖自给农业、村社土地或庄园工资，现金储蓄极少。城市中产阶层规模有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was distributed very unevenly. A small class of landowners, mine operators, merchants, and church institutions controlled large holdings of land, property, and precious-metal assets, while most rural households depended on subsistence cultivation, village lands, or hacienda wages and possessed little cash savings. The urban middle class remained limited.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业和矿业为基础的市场经济，国家财政高度依赖关税和特许收入；资本、信贷与基础设施薄弱，地方保护、军费压力和政治不稳限制了工业化发展
economicSystem.en: A market economy based on agriculture and mining, with state finances heavily dependent on tariffs and monopoly revenues; weak capital markets, limited credit and infrastructure, regional protectionism, military burdens, and political instability constrained industrialization

source.en: Internal indirect taxes and stamp duties
Salt duties, postal income, and miscellaneous fees

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库现金储备偏低，常因军费、债务、地方叛乱和海关收入波动而紧张。政府经常依靠预支关税、内外借款、拖欠军饷和非常征敛维持运转。
treasuryDescription.en: Cash reserves in the treasury were low and frequently strained by military costs, debt burdens, regional rebellions, and fluctuations in customs income. The government often relied on advances against customs revenues, domestic and foreign borrowing, arrears in military pay, and extraordinary exactions to continue functioning.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持政权、保障军费和争取债权人为主，偏重短期筹款而非系统性改革。政府努力恢复海关征收、压缩部分支出并寻求集中财政控制，但因政局反复和信用低下成效有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on regime survival, military funding, and appeasing creditors, emphasizing short-term revenue extraction over systematic reform. The government tried to restore customs collection, cut selected expenses, and centralize fiscal control, but repeated political upheaval and weak credit limited its success.

description.zh: 为军队主体，由常备营、地方守备部队和部分国民兵组成，装备以燧发枪和平滑膛火炮支援为主
description.en: The main body of the military, composed of regular battalions, local garrison troops, and some militia, armed mainly with flintlock muskets and supported by smoothbore artillery

description.zh: 在北部边疆和内陆机动作战中尤为重要，包括正规骑兵、龙骑兵和地方骑乘部队，适合追击、侦察与反游击作战
description.en: Especially important on the northern frontier and in mobile inland campaigning, including regular cavalry, dragoons, and regional mounted units suited for pursuit, scouting, and anti-guerrilla operations

description.zh: 负责野战炮、攻城炮和城防火炮，在首都、要塞和主要战场承担关键支援任务
description.en: Operated field guns, siege pieces, and fortress artillery, playing a key support role in the capital, fortifications, and major campaigns

description.zh: 规模较小，主要保护墨西哥湾与太平洋沿岸港口、执行巡逻、运输与有限海上防御任务
description.en: Relatively small in scale, tasked mainly with protecting Gulf and Pacific ports, conducting patrols, transport, and limited maritime defense

commanderInChief.zh: 总统阿纳斯塔西奥·布斯塔曼特

technology.zh: 武器技术总体处于19世纪前期水平，以燧发枪、刺刀、滑膛炮、骑兵冷兵器和传统攻城工事为主。部分装备依赖进口，军械制造和后勤补给能力有限，训练水平因部队和地区差异很大。
technology.en: Military technology was generally at an early nineteenth-century level, centered on flintlock muskets, bayonets, smoothbore cannon, cavalry sabers, and traditional fortification methods. Some equipment depended on imports, while domestic arms production and logistics were limited, and training standards varied greatly by unit and region.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自得克萨斯分离与其背后的美国扩张压力、北部边疆印第安袭扰、国内联邦派与中央派武装冲突、地方叛乱以及财政崩溃导致的军队失控风险。
threats.en: The chief threats were the secession of Texas and the expansionist pressure behind it from the United States, indigenous raids on the northern frontier, armed conflict between federalist and centralist factions, regional uprisings, and the risk of military disorder caused by fiscal collapse.

populationDescription.zh: 全国人口约七百万，分布极不均衡，主要集中在墨西哥谷地、中部高原、普埃布拉、瓜纳华托、哈利斯科、瓦哈卡和韦拉克鲁斯等传统核心地带；北部边疆地广人稀。人口由混血居民、原住民社群、欧洲裔少数及少量非洲裔后裔组成。
populationDescription.en: The national population was about seven million and very unevenly distributed, concentrated mainly in the Valley of Mexico, the central plateau, Puebla, Guanajuato, Jalisco, Oaxaca, Veracruz, and other traditional core regions, while the northern frontier remained sparsely settled. The population consisted of mestizos, indigenous communities, a European-descended minority, and a smaller number of people of partial African descent.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括军政人物、教会高层、大地产主、矿主与大商人；中层包括律师、医生、教师、下级官员、军官、手工业主和城市店主；下层则由村社农民、庄园劳工、佃农、城市工匠、日工、仆役和失业贫民构成。原住民村社在许多地区仍保持集体生活与地方习俗。
socialClasses.en: The upper class included military and political figures, senior clergy, large landowners, mine operators, and major merchants; the middle included lawyers, doctors, teachers, lesser officials, officers, master artisans, and urban shopkeepers; the lower classes consisted of village peasants, hacienda laborers, tenants, urban craftsmen, day laborers, servants, and the unemployed poor. Indigenous village communities in many regions still maintained collective life and local custom.

allies.zh: 1840年墨西哥没有可靠而稳定的正式大国盟友；与英国保持重要商业关系，并与西班牙在1836年承认独立后逐步恢复外交往来。对中南美邻国则多以谨慎观望为主。
allies.en: In 1840 Mexico had no dependable and stable formal great-power ally; it maintained important commercial ties with Britain and had begun restoring diplomatic relations with Spain after Spanish recognition of independence in 1836. Toward neighboring Latin American states it remained mostly cautious and watchful.

enemies.zh: 最主要的敌对对象是得克萨斯分离政权，以及在边界、移民和领土问题上日益对立的美国；国内各派地方军事集团也构成半内战式威胁。
enemies.en: Its principal hostile counterpart was the secessionist Texas regime, as well as the increasingly antagonistic United States over borders, migration, and territory; domestic regional military factions also posed a quasi-civil-war threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策核心是维护国家主权、争取国际承认、阻止得克萨斯独立合法化、平衡英美影响并尽可能保住北方边疆。由于财政和军力有限，墨西哥更多依赖谈判、法律主张和有限军事行动相结合的防御性外交。
foreignPolicy.en: The core of foreign policy was to defend national sovereignty, secure international recognition, prevent the legitimization of Texan independence, balance British and American influence, and preserve the northern frontier as far as possible. Because fiscal and military resources were limited, Mexico relied on a defensive diplomacy combining negotiation, legal claims, and limited military action.

keyInnovations.zh: 墨西哥在1840年尚未实现广泛工业化，但已接触蒸汽航运、近代印刷、改良采矿排水与冶炼技术、机械纺织品输入、近代测绘和更系统的军事工程。技术传播主要依赖进口设备、外国商人和矿业投资，而非本土大规模机器制造。
keyInnovations.en: By 1840 Mexico had not achieved broad industrialization, but it had been exposed to steam navigation, modern printing, improved mining drainage and smelting techniques, imported mechanized textiles, modern surveying, and more systematic military engineering. Technological diffusion depended mainly on imported equipment, foreign merchants, and mining investment rather than large-scale domestic machine production.

strengths.zh: 墨西哥拥有广阔领土、重要银矿资源、战略港口、深厚的天主教与城市文化传统，以及在西属美洲继承下来的行政与法律基础。其人口规模和资源禀赋使其具备成为地区强国的潜力。
strengths.en: Mexico possessed vast territory, important silver resources, strategic ports, deep Catholic and urban cultural traditions, and administrative and legal foundations inherited from Spanish America. Its population size and resource endowment gave it the potential to become a major regional power.

weaknesses.zh: 国家最大弱点是政局极端不稳、军人干政频繁、财政赤字严重、债务沉重、地方离心力强、北方边疆控制薄弱，以及交通基础设施不足。中央政府难以稳定征税和实施长期政策，直接削弱了战争与治理能力。
weaknesses.en: The state's greatest weaknesses were extreme political instability, chronic military intervention in politics, severe fiscal deficits, heavy debt, strong regional centrifugal forces, weak control of the northern frontier, and poor transport infrastructure. The central government struggled to tax consistently and pursue long-term policy, directly undermining both warfare and governance.

outlook.zh: 1840年的前景严峻。若不能恢复财政、整合军队并缓和中央与地方冲突，墨西哥将继续在分裂与外部压力中受损，尤其可能在北方领土问题上遭遇更大危机。但若能建立更稳定的宪政秩序并重振矿业与海关收入，仍有可能逐步恢复国家能力。
outlook.en: The outlook in 1840 was grim. Unless Mexico restored its finances, unified its armed forces, and eased the conflict between central and regional powers, it would continue to be damaged by fragmentation and outside pressure, especially in the northern territorial question. Yet if it could establish a more stable constitutional order and revive mining and customs revenues, a gradual recovery of state capacity remained possible.

description.zh: 独立后政局反复动荡的美洲共和国，面临德州分离与边疆压力。
description.en: A post-independence American republic marked by repeated instability, facing Texas secession and frontier pressure.

governmentForm.zh: 1833年宪法下的中央集权总统制共和国，由保守派主导，强调秩序、天主教和行政统一
governmentForm.en: A centralized presidential republic under the Constitution of 1833, dominated by conservatives and emphasizing order, Catholicism, and administrative unity

socialStructure.zh: 社会以大地产精英和城市显贵为上层，教士、军官与专业人士构成中上层，乡村佃农、小农、手工业者与城市平民为多数，南部边境存在马普切等原住民社会
socialStructure.en: Society was led by landed elites and urban notables, with clergy, officers, and professionals forming an upper middle stratum; tenant farmers, smallholders, artisans, and urban commoners made up the majority, while indigenous societies such as the Mapuche remained present on the southern frontier

rulingClass.zh: 保守派地主寡头、军政官僚、天主教高阶教士以及与国家结盟的商业精英
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of conservative landed oligarchs, military-administrative officials, senior Catholic clergy, and commercial elites aligned with the state

succession.zh: 总统由间接选举产生，政治上通常通过精英协商与有限选举完成权力交接
succession.en: The president was chosen through an indirect electoral system, with political succession typically managed through elite negotiation and limited elections

structure.zh: 中央政府由总统领导，国务部长分掌内政与外交、财政、战争与海军、司法与宗教事务；国会为两院制，但行政权在1840年占据明显主导地位
structure.en: The central government was led by the president, with ministers responsible for interior and foreign affairs, finance, war and navy, and justice and religious affairs; Congress was bicameral, but executive authority was clearly predominant in 1840

name.en: Ministry of Interior and Foreign Relations

function.zh: 管理国内行政、省级监督、警务协调、选举事务及对外关系
function.en: Managed domestic administration, provincial oversight, policing coordination, electoral matters, and foreign relations

function.zh: 负责海关、税收征管、预算编制、国债与国库管理
function.en: Oversaw customs, tax collection, budget preparation, public debt, and treasury management

function.zh: 统辖陆军、海军、边防要塞、军需与民兵体系
function.en: Supervised the army, navy, frontier forts, military supply, and militia systems

name.zh: 司法、宗教与公共教育部
name.en: Ministry of Justice, Worship, and Public Instruction

function.zh: 管理法院体系、监狱、国家与教会关系以及初等和中等教育事务
function.en: Managed the court system, prisons, state-church relations, and primary and secondary education

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为省、县和次级辖区，由中央任命的省长和地方官执行政策；地方自治有限，重要决策受圣地亚哥控制
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into provinces, departments, and smaller districts, where centrally appointed intendants and local officials implemented policy; local autonomy was limited, and major decisions were controlled from Santiago

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系承袭西班牙殖民法传统并逐步共和国化，结合成文法、总统法令和教会法影响；司法独立名义存在，但政治秩序优先
legalSystem.en: The legal system inherited Spanish colonial legal traditions while gradually adapting to republican rule, combining statutory law, presidential decrees, and the influence of canon law; judicial independence existed in principle, but political order took priority

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以海关关税、消费税、印花税和部分直接征收为主，国家财政高度依赖对外贸易口岸收入
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied primarily on customs duties, excises, stamp taxes, and limited direct levies, with state finances heavily dependent on revenues from foreign trade ports

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教和社会主导信仰，民间亦保留地方习俗与原住民传统元素
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the state religion and dominant social faith, though popular practice also retained local customs and indigenous elements

philosophy.zh: 政治思想以保守共和主义、天主教道德秩序和有限自由主义并存，强调稳定、权威与渐进改革
philosophy.en: Political thought combined conservative republicanism, Catholic moral order, and limited liberalism, emphasizing stability, authority, and gradual reform

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母书写西班牙语；国家文书、报刊与教育体系均以西班牙语为核心
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used to write Spanish; state documents, newspapers, and education were centered on Spanish

culturalAchievements.zh: 独立后智利在国家建构、宪政稳定、公共教育起步和报刊文化方面取得进展，圣地亚哥与瓦尔帕莱索成为文学、政治辩论和印刷出版中心
culturalAchievements.en: After independence, Chile made advances in state-building, constitutional stability, early public education, and print culture, with Santiago and Valparaiso becoming centers of literature, political debate, and publishing

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系罗曼语族的西班牙语占主导，南部和边疆地区并存马普敦贡等原住民语言
languageFamily.en: Spanish, an Indo-European Romance language, was dominant, while indigenous languages such as Mapudungun persisted in the south and frontier regions

gdpDescription.zh: 1840年的智利经济在拉丁美洲属较稳健类型，规模中等，依赖农业、畜牧、矿业和太平洋贸易。国家财政纪律较强，瓦尔帕莱索作为区域转口港的重要性日益上升
gdpDescription.en: In 1840 Chile had a comparatively robust Latin American economy of moderate size, reliant on agriculture, livestock, mining, and Pacific trade. Fiscal discipline was relatively strong, and Valparaiso was growing in importance as a regional entrepot port

mainIndustries.zh: 小麦与面粉生产、畜牧业、银矿与铜矿开采、皮革加工、葡萄酒、港口贸易、造船修理和手工业
mainIndustries.en: Wheat and flour production, livestock raising, silver and copper mining, leather processing, wine, port commerce, ship repair, and handicrafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口小麦、面粉、铜、银、皮革、牛脂和葡萄酒；进口纺织品、金属制品、工具、奢侈品、书籍和部分机械
tradeGoods.en: Exports included wheat, flour, copper, silver, hides, tallow, and wine; imports included textiles, metal goods, tools, luxury items, books, and some machinery

householdWealth.zh: 财富分配高度不均，大地产家族和商人拥有大部分土地、信贷与进口网络；多数农村家庭以自给与低现金收入维生
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal, with large landowning families and merchants controlling much of the land, credit, and import networks; most rural households lived through subsistence and low cash incomes

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换和出口导向为主的农业—矿业经济，国家通过关税、港务和财政审慎进行调控，但尚非工业化资本主义经济
economicSystem.en: It was an agriculture-and-mining economy oriented toward market exchange and exports, regulated by the state through tariffs, port administration, and fiscal prudence, but not yet an industrial capitalist economy

source.zh: 国内消费税与专卖收入
印花税、司法费用与行政收费
source.en: Stamp taxes, judicial fees, and administrative charges

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况较稳健，在拉丁美洲新兴共和国中以财政秩序和偿债信誉相对突出，但仍受贸易波动和出口市场变化影响
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was in comparatively sound condition; among the new Latin American republics, Chile stood out for fiscal order and creditworthiness, though it remained vulnerable to trade fluctuations and export market changes

fiscalPolicy.zh: 政府奉行保守财政政策，重视平衡预算、维持信用、限制无序借贷，并以海关收入支持国家建设和军备维持
fiscalPolicy.en: The government followed conservative fiscal policies, prioritizing balanced budgets, maintenance of credit, restraint in disorderly borrowing, and the use of customs revenue to support state-building and military maintenance

description.zh: 由步兵、骑兵、炮兵和边防驻军组成，主要任务是维持国内秩序、守卫北部与南部边境并支援阿劳卡尼亚方向的前沿据点
description.en: Consisted of infantry, cavalry, artillery, and frontier garrisons, tasked mainly with maintaining domestic order, guarding the northern and southern borders, and supporting forward posts toward Araucania

description.zh: 规模不大但对国家战略极为重要，负责沿海巡逻、港口防卫、运输和维护对太平洋航线的存在
description.en: Small in size but highly important strategically, it handled coastal patrols, port defense, transport, and maintenance of a presence on Pacific sea routes

description.zh: 由地方征集的受训公民和地方武装构成，在紧急情况下加强驻军、警备与边疆防务
description.en: Made up of locally raised trained citizens and provincial armed bodies, used in emergencies to reinforce garrisons, policing, and frontier defense

commanderInChief.zh: 总统何塞·华金·普列托

technology.zh: 装备以燧发枪、滑膛炮、骑兵冷兵器和木制风帆军舰为主，部分采用较新的炮兵训练和后勤制度，但总体仍属前工业时代军制向近代军队过渡阶段
technology.en: Equipment was based on flintlock muskets, smoothbore artillery, cavalry sabers, and wooden sailing warships, with some adoption of newer artillery training and logistics, though overall the military remained in transition from pre-industrial forces to a modern army

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与秘鲁—玻利维亚联邦战争后的地区紧张局势、阿根廷方向的不确定边界、南部马普切边疆摩擦以及海上贸易线安全问题
threats.en: Main threats came from regional tensions after the War against the Peru-Bolivian Confederation, uncertain borders toward Argentina, friction on the southern Mapuche frontier, and the security of maritime trade routes

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于中部谷地，从科金博至康塞普西翁之间最为稠密；北部较稀疏，南部边疆定居点有限且与原住民地区交错
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly in the central valley, densest between Coquimbo and Concepcion; the north was more sparsely settled, and the southern frontier had limited settlements interwoven with indigenous territories

socialClasses.zh: 上层为地主家族、商人、官员和高阶教士；中层包括军官、律师、医生、教师和熟练工匠；下层主要是佃农、农工、矿工、仆役和城市劳工，另有边疆原住民共同体保持相对独立社会结构
socialClasses.en: The upper class consisted of landed families, merchants, officials, and senior clergy; the middle strata included officers, lawyers, physicians, teachers, and skilled artisans; the lower classes were mainly tenant farmers, rural laborers, miners, servants, and urban workers, while frontier indigenous communities retained relatively autonomous social structures

allies.zh: 与英国保持重要商业与海上联系；在南美太平洋沿岸与部分反秘鲁—玻利维亚联邦势力有合作传统，但1840年并无正式强大军事同盟体系
allies.en: Chile maintained important commercial and maritime ties with Britain; along the Pacific coast of South America it had traditions of cooperation with forces opposed to the Peru-Bolivian Confederation, though in 1840 it had no strong formal military alliance system

enemies.zh: 无绝对固定宿敌，但与秘鲁、玻利维亚之间仍存战争余波与战略猜疑；与阿根廷的边界和安第斯南段影响力问题亦存在潜在摩擦
enemies.en: It had no single permanent archenemy, but lingering consequences of war and strategic suspicion remained with Peru and Bolivia; there was also potential friction with Argentina over borders and influence in the southern Andes

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护太平洋贸易、巩固独立、避免财政破产式战争、平衡周边国家并逐步扩展南部边疆控制为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on protecting Pacific commerce, consolidating independence, avoiding fiscally ruinous wars, balancing neighboring states, and gradually extending control over the southern frontier

keyInnovations.zh: 智利在1840年开始吸收蒸汽航运、近代港口管理、改良采矿技术、印刷传播和更系统的公共教育理念，但本土工业化仍十分有限
keyInnovations.en: By 1840 Chile was beginning to absorb steam navigation, modern port management, improved mining techniques, print dissemination, and more systematic ideas of public education, though domestic industrialization remained very limited

strengths.zh: 政治秩序较稳定，宪政框架相对稳固，财政管理优于多数周边新共和国，港口贸易活跃，中央政权对核心地区控制较强
strengths.en: Its strengths were relatively stable political order, a comparatively solid constitutional framework, better fiscal management than most neighboring new republics, active port commerce, and strong central control over the core regions

weaknesses.zh: 经济基础仍偏狭窄，过度依赖出口和海关收入，地区发展不均，社会贫富悬殊明显，南部边疆整合有限，工业基础薄弱
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a still narrow economic base, heavy dependence on exports and customs revenues, uneven regional development, marked social inequality, limited integration of the southern frontier, and a weak industrial base

outlook.zh: 若继续维持财政稳健、加强港口和教育建设并谨慎处理对外冲突，智利有望在19世纪中叶成为南美最稳定且最具行政能力的共和国之一
outlook.en: If it maintained fiscal prudence, strengthened ports and education, and handled external conflicts carefully, Chile was likely to become one of the most stable and administratively capable republics in South America by the mid-19th century

description.zh: 太平洋沿岸较为稳定的共和国，在安第斯南段逐渐扩张。
description.en: A relatively stable Pacific republic gradually expanding along the southern Andes.

governmentForm.zh: 君主立宪制下的世袭帝国，带有强烈中央集权色彩，并设有“调停权”皇权机制
governmentForm.en: A hereditary constitutional empire with strong centralizing tendencies, incorporating the imperial 'Moderating Power'

socialStructure.zh: 社会以大土地所有者、奴隶主精英和官僚为上层，城市自由民、工匠和商人为中层，广大自由贫民、佃农、混血民众与被奴役的非洲裔人口构成下层；地区差异显著，东北糖业区、东南咖啡扩张区和南部牧业区结构各异
socialStructure.en: Society was dominated by large landowners, slaveholding elites, and officials at the top, with urban freemen, artisans, and merchants in the middle, while poor free people, tenants, mixed-race populations, and enslaved Africans formed the lower strata; strong regional differences existed between the northeastern sugar zone, the expanding southeastern coffee zone, and the southern ranching districts

rulingClass.zh: 皇室、自由派与保守派政界精英、地方庄园主、咖啡与糖种植园主、高级军官及高等教士
rulingClass.en: The imperial family, Liberal and Conservative political elites, provincial landed oligarchs, coffee and sugar planters, senior military officers, and higher clergy

succession.zh: 布拉干萨王朝的世袭继承制，原则上实行长子优先；1840年佩德罗二世通过“成年宣告”提前亲政
succession.en: Hereditary succession within the House of Braganza, in principle by primogeniture; in 1840 Pedro II assumed full powers early through the 'Declaration of Majority'

structure.zh: 依据1824年宪法，中央政权由皇帝、国务会议、内阁大臣、参议院、众议院和司法体系组成。皇帝通过调停权在行政、立法与政治仲裁中拥有超越性影响，各省设省长与地方议会，但自治受到中央约束
structure.en: Under the Constitution of 1824, the central state consisted of the emperor, Council of State, cabinet ministers, Senate, Chamber of Deputies, and judiciary. Through the Moderating Power, the emperor exercised overarching influence in executive action, legislation, and political arbitration; provinces had presidents and local assemblies, but their autonomy was constrained by the center

function.zh: 负责内政、教育、教会事务、人口登记、公共秩序与省务协调
function.en: Handled internal administration, education, church affairs, civil registration, public order, and coordination with the provinces

function.zh: 管理海关、税收、国债、铸币、预算与国库
function.en: Managed customs, taxation, public debt, minting, budgeting, and the treasury

function.zh: 监督法院、检察、监狱、警务和法令执行
function.en: Oversaw courts, prosecution, prisons, policing, and execution of laws

function.zh: 负责陆军征募、军官体系、边防、兵站和军事行政
function.en: Administered army recruitment, officer corps, frontier defense, logistics, and military administration

function.zh: 负责舰队、造船、海军基地、海岸防御和海外交通保护
function.en: Managed the fleet, naval construction, dockyards, coastal defense, and protection of overseas communications

function.zh: 处理对外关系、条约、驻外使团和边界谈判
function.en: Handled foreign relations, treaties, overseas missions, and boundary negotiations

name.zh: 农业、商业与公共工程相关机构
name.en: Agriculture, Commerce, and Public Works Offices

function.zh: 以分散机构形式管理农业促进、商业统计、道路、港口与基础设施事务
function.en: Managed agricultural promotion, commercial statistics, roads, ports, and infrastructure through dispersed offices rather than a fully separate ministry

localAdmin.zh: 全国分为各省，由皇帝任命的省长主持行政，省议会负责有限地方立法；下辖市镇和教区，地方权力常掌握在治安法官、地主家族和市政议会手中
localAdmin.en: The empire was divided into provinces governed by imperial appointees, while provincial assemblies exercised limited local legislation; below them were municipalities and parishes, where authority often rested with justices of the peace, landholding families, and town councils

legalSystem.zh: 以1824年宪法为根本，结合葡萄牙法传统、帝国立法与成文刑商法规。形式上存在代议与法治框架，但奴隶制、地方庇护关系和行政干预削弱了法律平等
legalSystem.en: The legal order rested on the Constitution of 1824, Portuguese legal traditions, imperial statutes, and codified criminal and commercial norms. A representative and rule-based framework existed in form, but slavery, local patronage networks, and executive interference limited legal equality

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖进出口关税，尤其是海关收入，同时征收印花税、消费税、土地与产业相关杂税以及部分省级摊派；税制对外贸依赖极强
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal income depended chiefly on import and export duties, especially customs revenue, alongside stamp taxes, excises, assorted land and business levies, and some provincial assessments; the tax system was highly dependent on external trade

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教，国家通过保教权对教会拥有较强控制；同时民间广泛存在非洲裔宗教实践、民间天主教、土著信仰及有限的新教商旅社群
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the state religion, with the crown exercising substantial control over the church through patronage rights; popular religion also included Afro-Brazilian practices, folk Catholicism, indigenous beliefs, and small Protestant merchant communities

philosophy.zh: 政治思想受葡萄牙启蒙、自由主义宪政、保守秩序观与温和实用主义影响；精英阶层强调君主制、领土统一和社会等级，同时吸纳部分科学理性与进步观念
philosophy.en: Political thought was shaped by the Portuguese Enlightenment, liberal constitutionalism, conservative ideas of order, and moderate pragmatism; elites emphasized monarchy, territorial unity, and social hierarchy while selectively adopting scientific rationalism and notions of progress

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母书写葡萄牙语，官方文书与教育体系均以葡萄牙语为主
writingSystem.en: The empire used the Latin alphabet to write Portuguese, which dominated official documentation and education

culturalAchievements.zh: 1840年前后的巴西文化成就主要体现在民族文学、帝国礼仪、宗教建筑、肖像绘画和音乐传统的形成上。浪漫主义开始为帝国塑造独特身份，强调自然、印第安主题和祖国想象；里约热内卢逐渐成为印刷、戏剧与宫廷文化中心
culturalAchievements.en: Brazilian cultural achievement around 1840 lay chiefly in the formation of national literature, imperial ceremonial culture, religious architecture, portrait painting, and musical traditions. Early Romanticism began to shape a distinct imperial identity, emphasizing nature, Indianist themes, and patriotic imagination; Rio de Janeiro was emerging as a center of print, theater, and court culture

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系罗曼语族，以葡萄牙语为主，另有非洲语言、土著语言和移民语言在地方并存
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Romance branch, with Portuguese dominant, alongside African, indigenous, and immigrant languages in local use

gdpDescription.zh: 1840年的巴西帝国经济规模在拉丁美洲居前列，依托广阔领土、奴隶制种植园和出口农业。经济重心正从传统东北糖业转向东南咖啡带，内需市场有限，工业化尚处萌芽阶段，整体属于以初级产品出口为主的农业型帝国经济
gdpDescription.en: In 1840 the Brazilian Empire possessed one of the largest economies in Latin America, built on vast territory, slave plantation labor, and export agriculture. The center of gravity was shifting from the traditional northeastern sugar zone toward the southeastern coffee belt; the domestic market remained limited, industrialization was only embryonic, and the economy was fundamentally an agrarian imperial export system

mainIndustries.zh: 咖啡种植、糖业、棉花、烟草、畜牧业、皮革、木材、黄金与钻石的残余开采、沿海航运、手工业加工与食品酿造
mainIndustries.en: Coffee cultivation, sugar production, cotton, tobacco, ranching, hides and leather, timber, residual gold and diamond mining, coastal shipping, artisanal processing, and food and drink production

tradeGoods.zh: 主要出口咖啡、糖、棉花、烟草、皮革、染料木和少量矿产；主要进口纺织品、金属制品、机械、奢侈品、盐、面粉及部分军需品
tradeGoods.en: Major exports included coffee, sugar, cotton, tobacco, hides, dyewood, and small quantities of minerals; key imports included textiles, metal goods, machinery, luxury items, salt, flour, and some military supplies

name.zh: 帝国雷亚尔（米尔雷斯记账）

householdWealth.zh: 财富分配极不均衡。大庄园、奴隶、土地与城市房产集中于少数精英家庭；普通自由民家庭多为低积累状态，农村贫民和奴隶基本不拥有可观财富
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was extremely unequal. Plantations, slaves, land, and urban property were concentrated in a small elite, while most free households had low accumulated assets and rural poor and enslaved people possessed virtually no recognized wealth

economicSystem.zh: 以奴隶制种植园经济、出口导向农业和港口贸易为核心的混合市场经济，国家通过关税、特许和基础设施支持维持统合，但地方寡头在资源配置中作用巨大
economicSystem.en: A mixed market economy centered on slave plantation production, export-oriented agriculture, and port commerce, with the state maintaining cohesion through tariffs, concessions, and infrastructure support, while provincial oligarchs played a major role in resource allocation

source.zh: 国有资产、邮政与杂项收费
source.en: State properties, postal income, and miscellaneous fees

source.zh: 借款与财政性周转收入

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况在1840年相对稳定但并不宽裕，依赖关税与信用维持现金流。由于先前摄政时期的地方叛乱和军事开支，财政仍承受债务与地区治安成本压力
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury in 1840 was relatively stable but not abundant, relying on customs and credit to maintain liquidity. Because of provincial revolts and military expenditures during the regency, public finances still bore pressure from debt and the cost of internal pacification

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以恢复秩序、保持信用、优先保障军费与行政运转为核心，避免激进税改，继续依赖海关收入并在必要时举借内外债
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on restoring order, preserving public credit, and prioritizing military and administrative continuity, avoiding radical tax reform while continuing to rely on customs receipts and resorting to domestic and foreign borrowing when necessary

description.zh: 帝国军力核心，包括正规步兵、骑兵、炮兵、地方守备队及边境驻军，主要任务是镇压内乱、维护省份秩序并防范南部边境冲突
description.en: The core of imperial military power, including regular infantry, cavalry, artillery, local garrisons, and frontier detachments; its main role was suppressing internal revolts, maintaining provincial order, and guarding the southern frontier

description.zh: 在拉丁美洲具较强地位，拥有风帆战舰、巡航舰、炮舰与海军陆战力量，负责海岸防御、河口控制、护航和对南大西洋航线的存在展示
description.en: A relatively strong force by Latin American standards, possessing sailing warships, cruisers, gun vessels, and marine elements; it was tasked with coastal defense, estuary control, convoying, and demonstrating presence in the South Atlantic

description.zh: 由地方精英主导组织，用于地方治安、紧急动员和支援正规军，但训练和统一指挥有限
description.en: Organized under the influence of local elites for security, emergency mobilization, and support to regular troops, though training and unified command were limited

commanderInChief.zh: 巴西皇帝佩德罗二世（名义统帅），实际由战争部与高级将领执行指挥
commanderInChief.en: Emperor Pedro II of Brazil (nominal commander-in-chief), with practical command exercised through the Ministry of War and senior generals

technology.zh: 军备处于前工业到早期工业过渡阶段，主要使用燧发枪、滑膛火炮、骑兵冷兵器和木制风帆战舰，部分军械与海军装备依赖英国等外国供应
technology.en: Military technology stood at the transition from pre-industrial to early industrial forms, relying mainly on flintlock muskets, smoothbore artillery, cavalry sabers, and wooden sailing warships, with some arms and naval material dependent on foreign—especially British—suppliers

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自国内省级叛乱、地方军阀化倾向、南部拉普拉塔地区局势，以及奴隶社会内部的不稳定；外部全面入侵风险较低，但边界与河口争端长期存在
threats.en: The principal threats came from provincial rebellions, tendencies toward local militarization, instability in the southern La Plata region, and tensions within a slave society; the risk of full-scale foreign invasion was low, but border and estuarine disputes persisted

populationDescription.zh: 人口约七百万，构成高度多元，包括葡裔白人、巴西本地白人、非洲裔奴隶与自由民、混血群体以及数量下降但仍重要的土著社群。人口主要分布于大西洋沿岸和东南、东北农业带，内陆广大地区仍稀疏
populationDescription.en: The population of about seven million was highly diverse, including Portuguese-born whites, Brazilian-born whites, enslaved and free people of African descent, mixed-race populations, and diminished yet still significant indigenous communities. Settlement was concentrated along the Atlantic coast and the southeastern and northeastern agricultural belts, while much of the interior remained sparsely populated

socialClasses.zh: 上层为皇室、贵族、教士、高官与大种植园主；中层包括军官、律师、商人、店主、手工业者和受教育自由民；下层则是自由贫民、佃户、城市劳工、士兵、奴隶与边缘化土著群体。奴隶制深刻塑造了社会流动与权利结构
socialClasses.en: The upper class consisted of the imperial family, titled nobility, clergy, high officials, and large plantation owners; the middle strata included officers, lawyers, merchants, shopkeepers, artisans, and educated free people; the lower strata comprised poor freemen, tenants, urban laborers, soldiers, enslaved people, and marginalized indigenous communities. Slavery profoundly shaped social mobility and the structure of rights

allies.zh: 与英国保持最重要的商业和金融合作关系；与葡萄牙维持王朝和文化联系；在南美洲寻求同乌拉圭友好并对拉普拉塔局势施加影响
allies.en: Brazil's most important commercial and financial relationship was with Britain; it maintained dynastic and cultural ties with Portugal; in South America it sought friendly relations with Uruguay and influence over the politics of the La Plata basin

enemies.zh: 1840年并无欧洲列强意义上的正式全面敌国，但与阿根廷邦联在乌拉圭及河口势力问题上存在深刻战略竞争，并对跨境叛乱和边界摩擦保持警惕
enemies.en: In 1840 Brazil had no formal great-power enemy in the European sense, but it faced deep strategic rivalry with the Argentine Confederation over Uruguay and influence in the estuary region, while remaining alert to cross-border rebellions and frontier frictions

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护帝国领土完整、确保南大西洋航运、压制拉普拉塔地区敌对势力、维持对英贸易和争取国际承认为核心。政府倾向务实平衡，避免与欧洲强国直接冲突
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving imperial territorial integrity, securing South Atlantic shipping, checking hostile influence in the La Plata region, maintaining trade with Britain, and consolidating international recognition. The government preferred pragmatic balancing and avoided direct confrontation with European great powers

keyInnovations.zh: 巴西在1840年尚未成为工业创新中心，但已吸收蒸汽航运、现代印刷、改良港口设施、较系统的炮舰与船坞管理、测绘和远洋商业网络等技术要素。技术传播主要依赖英国输入和沿海城市精英采用
keyInnovations.en: Brazil in 1840 was not a major center of industrial innovation, but it had begun absorbing steam navigation, modern printing, improved port facilities, more systematic naval dockyard administration, surveying methods, and long-distance commercial networks. Technological diffusion came chiefly through British imports and adoption by coastal urban elites

strengths.zh: 疆域广阔、资源丰富、港口体系成熟、对外贸易活跃，君主制在1840年重新获得合法性并有望结束摄政时期动荡；海军在地区内具分量，国家统一程度高于多数西班牙美洲新兴国家
strengths.en: Its strengths included immense territory, abundant resources, established ports, active foreign trade, and a monarchy that in 1840 regained legitimacy and could help end regency-era instability; the navy carried regional weight, and the empire was more territorially cohesive than many post-independence Spanish American states

weaknesses.zh: 奴隶制经济造成社会撕裂和国际压力，财政过度依赖海关，交通和行政覆盖不足，地方寡头势力强大，内陆开发有限，教育水平低，工业基础薄弱
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses lay in a slave-based economy that produced social fracture and international pressure, excessive dependence on customs revenue, limited transport and administrative reach, strong provincial oligarchies, underdeveloped interior regions, low educational attainment, and a weak industrial base

outlook.zh: 若佩德罗二世能够成功稳定中央权威、平衡自由派与保守派、加强省份控制并利用咖啡出口增长，巴西帝国将在19世纪中期成为南美最稳固的国家之一；但长期前景仍受奴隶制、地区不平衡和外贸依赖所制约
outlook.en: If Pedro II could successfully stabilize central authority, balance Liberals and Conservatives, strengthen control over the provinces, and capitalize on rising coffee exports, the Brazilian Empire was positioned to become one of the most stable states in mid-19th-century South America; yet its long-term prospects remained constrained by slavery, regional imbalance, and dependence on foreign trade

description.zh: 南美最大的君主制国家，1840年佩德罗二世提前亲政以稳定局势。
description.en: The largest monarchy in South America, where Pedro II assumed power early in 1840 to stabilize the realm.

ruler.zh: 何塞·米格尔·德·贝拉斯科

governmentForm.zh: 由军人强人、地方精英与法定共和机构共同维系的不稳定中央集权共和国
governmentForm.en: An unstable centralized republic sustained jointly by military strongmen, regional elites, and formal republican institutions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以克里奥尔人与梅斯蒂索政治精英居上层，印第安社群构成多数农村人口，另有城市手工业者、矿工、教士、地主与少量商人阶层；地区差异显著，高原与山谷政治联系强于边缘低地。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by Creole and mestizo political elites, while Indigenous communities formed the majority of the rural population; there were also urban artisans, miners, clergy, landowners, and a small merchant stratum. Regional divisions were pronounced, with the highlands and valleys more politically integrated than the peripheral lowlands.

rulingClass.zh: 军官集团、地主家族、城市法学家与官僚、以及与教会和矿业相连的地方名流
rulingClass.en: Military officers, landed families, urban lawyers and bureaucrats, and local notables tied to the Church and mining

succession.zh: 名义上依宪法通过选举和总统交接，实际上常受政变、军队拥立与派系妥协左右
succession.en: Nominally through constitutional election and presidential transfer, but in practice often determined by coups, army backing, and factional compromise

structure.zh: 1840年的玻利维亚在秘鲁-玻利维亚邦联解体后重新确立共和国框架，中央政府设总统、国务部、国会与高等司法机构，但政局高度不稳，省级权力常受军区首领和地方寡头支配。
structure.en: In 1840 Bolivia reestablished a republican framework after the collapse of the Peru-Bolivian Confederation, with a president, ministries of state, a congress, and higher courts, but politics remained highly unstable and provincial authority was often dominated by military commanders and local oligarchs.

function.zh: 管理国内行政、地方官任命、治安、人口登记与政府公文传达
function.en: Managed internal administration, appointment of local officials, public order, population records, and transmission of government directives

function.zh: 负责税收征解、关税、国库、债务、铸币与财政预算
function.en: Oversaw tax collection, customs duties, the treasury, debt, coinage, and the state budget

function.zh: 统辖陆军、征募、军需、边防驻军与镇压叛乱行动
function.en: Directed the army, recruitment, logistics, frontier garrisons, and suppression of rebellions

function.zh: 处理与秘鲁、智利、阿根廷、巴西等邻国的外交事务、条约与使节往来
function.en: Handled diplomacy, treaties, and missions with neighboring states such as Peru, Chile, Argentina, and Brazil

name.en: Ministry of Justice and Ecclesiastical Affairs

function.zh: 管理法院体系、法令执行以及国家与天主教会的关系
function.en: Administered the court system, enforcement of laws, and relations between the state and the Catholic Church

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为若干部，部下设省、州或县及市镇。地方行政名义上由中央任命的长官和市政机构承担，但偏远地区常由地主、原住民社群领袖、驻军军官或教会实际调解治理。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into departments, then provinces, districts or cantons, and municipalities. Local administration was nominally carried out by centrally appointed prefects and municipal bodies, but in remote areas actual governance was often mediated by landowners, Indigenous community leaders, army officers, or the Church.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系继承西班牙殖民法传统并吸收共和宪制原则，民刑审判由地方法院和高等法院执行；在农村和印第安社群内部，习惯法和地方调解仍广泛存在。
legalSystem.en: The legal system inherited Spanish colonial legal traditions while incorporating republican constitutional principles. Civil and criminal justice was administered by local and higher courts; in rural areas and Indigenous communities, customary law and local arbitration remained widespread.

taxationSystem.zh: 国家收入主要依赖海关转口收入、矿业税、国内间接税、印花税、酒类与消费税，以及部分直接征收。财政征收效率低，逃税、地区扣留和军费挤占普遍存在。
taxationSystem.en: State revenue relied chiefly on customs and transit duties, mining taxes, domestic indirect taxes, stamp duties, liquor and consumption taxes, and some direct assessments. Collection efficiency was low, and tax evasion, regional retention of funds, and military diversion were common.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教占主导地位，并与安第斯原住民宗教习俗长期交融；国家政治生活中教会仍具重要影响。
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was dominant and had long blended with Andean Indigenous religious practices; the Church still held major influence in public and political life.

philosophy.zh: 公共思想以天主教道德、伊比利亚法政传统、共和主义、军人荣誉观和有限的自由主义宪政观念并存。
philosophy.en: Public thought combined Catholic morality, Iberian legal-political traditions, republicanism, military notions of honor, and limited liberal constitutional ideas.

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写系统为拉丁字母西班牙文；克丘亚语、艾马拉语等原住民语言多以口传为主，亦可用拉丁字母记录。
writingSystem.en: The official writing system was Spanish in the Latin alphabet; Indigenous languages such as Quechua and Aymara were primarily oral, though they could also be written in Latin script.

culturalAchievements.zh: 玻利维亚延续上秘鲁殖民时期的宗教建筑、银矿城镇文化、法律教育与巴洛克艺术传统；苏克雷和波托西仍是学术、教会音乐、法学与行政文化的重要中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Bolivia preserved the religious architecture, mining-town culture, legal learning, and baroque artistic traditions of colonial Upper Peru; Sucre and Potosi remained important centers of scholarship, church music, jurisprudence, and administrative culture.

languageFamily.zh: 官方语言属印欧语系罗曼语族的西班牙语；主要民间语言还包括克丘亚语和艾马拉语，分别属于克丘亚语系与艾马拉语系。
languageFamily.en: The official language was Spanish, an Indo-European Romance language; major vernacular languages also included Quechua and Aymara, belonging to the Quechuan and Aymaran language families respectively.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在南美属中下水平，主体仍为自给农业、社区生产与区域市场交换。银矿与相关运输贸易虽仍重要，但已远不及殖民时代高峰；政治动荡和战争破坏了财政与商业信心。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was of lower-middle scale by South American standards, still based mainly on subsistence agriculture, communal production, and regional market exchange. Silver mining and associated transport trade remained important but were far below their colonial peak; political instability and war had damaged fiscal and commercial confidence.

mainIndustries.zh: 高原农业、畜牧业、银矿开采、少量铜与金矿、骡运贸易、手工纺织、酿酒与地方手工业
mainIndustries.en: Highland agriculture, pastoralism, silver mining, limited copper and gold extraction, mule transport trade, handicraft textiles, distilling, and local artisanal manufacture

tradeGoods.zh: 出口银、金鸡纳、骡马、羊驼和骆马制品、部分矿产与手工品；输入纺织品、金属制成品、武器、纸张、奢侈品和部分沿海转运消费品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included silver, cinchona, mules, alpaca and llama products, some minerals, and handicrafts; imports included textiles, metal manufactures, arms, paper, luxury goods, and coastal transshipped consumer goods

householdWealth.zh: 多数家庭财富很低，以土地使用权、牲畜、农具和社区互助网络为主要资产；城市精英与矿业、商业家族拥有明显更高的白银、地产与债权积累。
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed very little wealth, with land-use rights, livestock, tools, and communal support networks as their main assets; urban elites and mining or commercial families held much larger stores of silver, real estate, and credit claims.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业和矿业为基础的早期共和混合经济，国家对关税、铸币与部分矿税有强影响，但地方市场、社区生产和私人商人网络在实际运行中更为关键。
economicSystem.en: An early republican mixed economy based on agriculture and mining, in which the state exerted strong influence over tariffs, minting, and some mining taxation, but local markets, communal production, and private merchant networks were more decisive in everyday practice.

source.zh: 矿业税与铸币相关收入
印花税、司法收费与杂项收入
source.en: Stamp duties, judicial fees, and miscellaneous income

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备有限，且部分属于账面应收或已被指定用于军饷、债务与紧急调拨。现金储备受政局变化、边境贸易波动和征收困难影响很大。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury reserves were limited, and part of them existed only as expected receipts or funds already earmarked for army pay, debt, and emergency transfers. Available cash was heavily affected by political instability, border trade fluctuations, and collection difficulties.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 政府财政政策以维持军队、保障中央权威和恢复战后财政秩序为主，依靠提高关税、追缴欠税和临时借款，但长期结构脆弱，难以形成稳定平衡预算。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy focused on maintaining the army, preserving central authority, and restoring postwar order through higher customs duties, recovery of arrears, and temporary borrowing, but the long-term structure remained too weak to sustain a stable balanced budget.

description.zh: 军队核心力量，多由高原与山谷征募而来，装备以燧发枪、刺刀和少量旧式制服为主，既承担国防也参与国内权力斗争。
description.en: The core arm of the military, recruited largely from the highlands and valleys and equipped mainly with flintlock muskets, bayonets, and limited old uniforms; it served both national defense and internal political struggles.

description.zh: 用于快速机动、追击与边境巡逻，在内战和地方叛乱中十分重要，依赖地方马匹资源与军官私人网络。
description.en: Used for rapid movement, pursuit, and frontier patrols, cavalry was especially important in civil conflicts and provincial rebellions and depended on local horse supplies and officers' personal networks.

description.zh: 规模不大，以旧式野战炮和要塞炮为主，主要部署在关键城市和战略通道。
description.en: Small in scale, with mostly older field and fortress guns, deployed mainly in key cities and strategic corridors.

name.zh: 国民民兵与地方守备队

description.zh: 由地方当局和社区组织，在紧急情况下动员，用于维持秩序、守卫城镇和支援正规军。
description.en: Organized by local authorities and communities and mobilized in emergencies to maintain order, guard towns, and support the regular army.

commanderInChief.zh: 总统何塞·米格尔·德·贝拉斯科为宪法上的武装力量统帅
commanderInChief.en: President Jose Miguel de Velasco as constitutional commander-in-chief of the armed forces

technology.zh: 军备处于前工业化与早期工业化过渡阶段，主要使用燧发枪、冷兵器、旧式火炮和骡马运输，后勤薄弱，军械多依赖进口或旧库存。
technology.en: Military technology stood at the transition between pre-industrial and early industrial conditions, relying chiefly on flintlock muskets, edged weapons, older artillery, and mule transport; logistics were weak and matériel depended heavily on imports or old stocks.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自国内军人政变、地方叛乱、邦联解体后的政治余波，以及与秘鲁、智利和阿根廷边境相关的战略不确定性。
threats.en: The chief threats came from domestic military coups, regional rebellions, the political aftershocks of the confederation's collapse, and strategic uncertainty on frontiers involving Peru, Chile, and Argentina.

populationDescription.zh: 人口以安第斯高原和谷地为主，原住民人口占多数，集中于农村社区；城市人口较少，主要聚集在苏克雷、波托西、拉巴斯和科恰班巴等历史行政与商业中心。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the Andean highlands and valleys, with Indigenous peoples forming the majority and living chiefly in rural communities; the urban population was relatively small and centered in historic administrative and commercial towns such as Sucre, Potosi, La Paz, and Cochabamba.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为军政精英、地主、矿主和教会高层；中层包括律师、商人、低级官员、军官和熟练工匠；下层主要是印第安农民、佃户、矿工、苦役劳动者和城市贫民。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of military-political elites, landowners, mine owners, and senior clergy; the middle strata included lawyers, merchants, lower officials, officers, and skilled artisans; the lower strata were mainly Indigenous peasants, tenants, miners, laborers, and the urban poor.

allies.zh: 1840年玻利维亚缺乏稳定而可靠的正式盟友，仅与部分邻国维持务实接触，并寻求避免新的地区战争。
allies.en: In 1840 Bolivia lacked stable and reliable formal allies, maintaining only pragmatic contacts with some neighboring states while seeking to avoid a new regional war.

enemies.zh: 并无持久的绝对敌国，但与秘鲁在邦联解体后的关系紧张，对智利和阿根廷也保持高度戒备；国内敌对派系往往比国外对手更直接危险。
enemies.en: It had no permanent absolute enemy, but relations with Peru were tense after the confederation's collapse, and it remained highly wary of Chile and Argentina; domestic rival factions were often more immediately dangerous than foreign opponents.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以保存主权、争取国际承认、避免再次卷入大规模安第斯战争、并在邻国间维持有限平衡为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving sovereignty, securing international recognition, avoiding renewed large-scale Andean war, and maintaining a limited balance among neighboring states.

keyInnovations.zh: 玻利维亚本土技术整体仍偏传统，但已接触近代铸币、矿山排水与选矿改良、印刷传播、共和国文书行政，以及经由进口获得的火器与金属制品。
keyInnovations.en: Bolivia's domestic technological base remained largely traditional, but it had adopted modern minting, some improvements in mine drainage and ore processing, print communication, republican bureaucratic paperwork, and imported firearms and metal goods.

strengths.zh: 拥有高地人口基础、深厚的殖民行政传统、重要矿产资源和安第斯腹地战略位置；社会在地方层面具有较强社区组织能力。
strengths.en: It possessed a substantial highland population base, a deep colonial administrative tradition, important mineral resources, and a strategic Andean interior position; at the local level society also retained strong communal organization.

weaknesses.zh: 最大弱点是政变频繁、财政脆弱、交通困难、地区分裂严重、出口基础狭窄且国家权威对边缘地区控制有限。
weaknesses.en: Its greatest weaknesses were frequent coups, fragile finances, difficult transport, severe regional fragmentation, a narrow export base, and limited state control over peripheral territories.

outlook.zh: 短期前景不稳。若总统能够压制军人派系、恢复税收和重建与邻国的正常关系，国家仍有机会依靠矿业和高原农业维持独立发展；若内斗继续，玻利维亚将长期陷于财政危机与政治轮替。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was unstable. If the president could restrain military factions, restore revenue, and rebuild normal relations with neighboring states, the country still had a chance to sustain independent development through mining and highland agriculture; if internal conflict continued, Bolivia would remain trapped in fiscal crisis and recurrent political turnover.

description.zh: 高原共和国，在联邦瓦解与军人政治的背景下局势不稳。
description.en: A highland republic unsettled by military politics and the collapse of confederative union.

ruler.zh: 胡安·曼努埃尔·德·罗萨斯

rulerTitle.zh: 布宜诺斯艾利斯省长兼邦联对外关系总负责人
rulerTitle.en: Governor of Buenos Aires and in charge of the Confederation's foreign relations

governmentForm.zh: 以省份联盟为基础、由布宜诺斯艾利斯主导的联邦共和体制
governmentForm.en: A federal republican confederation dominated by Buenos Aires

socialStructure.zh: 社会由大地产主与地方考迪罗、城市商人和官员、乡村高乔牧民、小农、手工业者、自由有色人、原住民社群与少量奴隶及前奴隶组成。沿海港口社会与内陆省份社会差异显著，政治忠诚多围绕地方领袖与联邦—统一派分裂展开。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of large landowners and provincial caudillos, urban merchants and officials, rural gaucho herdsmen, small farmers, artisans, free people of color, indigenous communities, and a small number of slaves and former slaves. Marked differences existed between the littoral port society and the inland provinces, and political loyalties often revolved around local strongmen and the Federalist–Unitarian divide.

rulingClass.zh: 布宜诺斯艾利斯的牧场主精英、出口商人、海关利益集团以及与罗萨斯结盟的各省联邦派考迪罗
rulingClass.en: The estanciero elite of Buenos Aires, export merchants, customs-interest groups, and Federalist provincial caudillos allied with Rosas

succession.zh: 名义上通过省级选举与代表机关任命，实际上依赖地方实力、精英协商与武装支持
succession.en: Nominally through provincial election and appointment by representative bodies, but in practice dependent on local power, elite negotiation, and armed backing

structure.zh: 1840年的邦联并非高度制度化的中央国家，而是由各省组成的松散政治共同体。布宜诺斯艾利斯省政府控制主要海关收入，并代表各省处理大部分外交事务。各省保留广泛自治权，军事与财政能力高度地方化，国家机构规模有限。
structure.en: In 1840 the Confederation was not a highly institutionalized central state but a loose political community of provinces. The government of Buenos Aires controlled the principal customs revenues and handled most foreign affairs on behalf of the provinces. The provinces retained broad autonomy, while military and fiscal capacity remained highly localized and the state apparatus was limited in size.

name.zh: 布宜诺斯艾利斯政府与总督府
name.en: Government and Governor's Office of Buenos Aires

function.zh: 统筹行政命令、人事任免、对内治安与与各省领袖协调，是罗萨斯政权的核心枢纽
function.en: Coordinated executive orders, appointments, internal security, and liaison with provincial leaders; it was the core hub of Rosas's regime

name.en: Office of Foreign and Confederation Affairs

function.zh: 处理对外条约、驻外往来、河运与封锁问题，并代表邦联与外国列强及邻国交涉
function.en: Handled foreign treaties, diplomatic correspondence, river navigation and blockade issues, and represented the Confederation before foreign powers and neighboring states

function.zh: 征收布宜诺斯艾利斯港口进出口关税，是财政收入最重要来源
function.en: Collected import and export duties at the port of Buenos Aires, the single most important source of public revenue

function.zh: 管理岁入、债务、军费拨付、官员薪俸与公共支付
function.en: Managed revenue, debt, military disbursements, official salaries, and public payments

function.zh: 组织省级与邦联军队、边境防务、骑兵征集与军需补给
function.en: Organized provincial and confederate forces, frontier defense, cavalry mobilization, and military supply

function.zh: 维持城市秩序、监视反对派、执行审查与配合马索尔卡等政治动员网络
function.en: Maintained urban order, monitored opponents, enforced censorship, and worked alongside political mobilization networks such as the Mazorca

function.zh: 处理法院、档案、公证、商事纠纷与刑事案件文书
function.en: Handled courts, archives, notarial records, commercial disputes, and criminal case documentation

function.zh: 由各省总督、议会或顾问机构分别管理地方税收、民兵、司法和农村行政
function.en: Administered local taxation, militias, justice, and rural governance under provincial governors, legislatures, or advisory bodies

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以省为单位，各省总督拥有广泛权力，县镇与乡村地区依靠治安法官、教区结构、民兵首领与地方名流维持统治。边疆地区往往以军事据点和与原住民部族的协议管理。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was organized by province, with governors wielding extensive authority. Districts, towns, and rural areas were governed through magistrates, parish structures, militia leaders, and local notables. Frontier zones were often managed through military posts and negotiated arrangements with indigenous groups.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系混合西班牙殖民法传统、地方条例、教会法残余和战时行政命令。正式上仍承袭西班牙法理与革命后法令，但政治案件常受行政权与警务力量强烈影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Spanish colonial legal traditions, provincial ordinances, residual canon-law influences, and wartime executive decrees. Formally it still rested on inherited Spanish jurisprudence and post-independence legislation, but political cases were heavily shaped by executive power and police influence.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以布宜诺斯艾利斯海关关税为核心，辅以消费税、印花和少量直接税。内陆各省自行征收通行税、地方案税和畜牧相关税费，财政分散而不均衡。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation centered on Buenos Aires customs duties, supplemented by excises, stamps, and limited direct taxes. Inland provinces collected their own transit dues, local levies, and livestock-related taxes, producing a fragmented and unequal fiscal structure.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为主导宗教，教会在婚姻、教育、节庆与社会规范中仍具重要地位，但国家对教会事务拥有较大干预权。
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the dominant religion, and the Church retained an important role in marriage, education, festivals, and social norms, though the state exercised considerable influence over ecclesiastical affairs.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想主要围绕联邦主义、地方自治、天主教秩序观与反统一派集权展开；同时自由主义、启蒙思想和浪漫民族主义在流亡者与部分城市知识阶层中持续传播。
philosophy.en: Political thought centered on federalism, provincial autonomy, a Catholic vision of order, and opposition to Unitarian centralism; at the same time, liberalism, Enlightenment ideas, and Romantic nationalism continued to circulate among exiles and segments of the urban educated class.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母书写西班牙语；官方文书、报刊、教会记录和商业账册均以西班牙语为主，部分边疆与地方社会亦使用原住民语言口传传统。
writingSystem.en: Spanish written in the Latin alphabet was used; official documents, newspapers, church records, and commercial accounts were predominantly in Spanish, while oral traditions in indigenous languages persisted in frontier and local societies.

culturalAchievements.zh: 里奥普拉塔地区形成了鲜明的高乔文化、牧歌传统、马黛茶习俗、民间歌谣与骑术文化。布宜诺斯艾利斯的印刷业和政治报刊活跃，联邦派象征政治、红色徽记与公共仪式塑造了强烈的时代风格。
culturalAchievements.en: The Río de la Plata region developed a distinctive gaucho culture, pastoral traditions, maté customs, folk song, and horsemanship. Printing and political journalism were active in Buenos Aires, while Federalist symbolism, red insignia, and public ritual gave the period a strong visual and political character.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属印欧语系罗曼语族的西班牙语；同时存在马普切语、克丘亚语、瓜拉尼语等原住民语言影响。
languageFamily.en: The principal language was Spanish, an Indo-European Romance language, alongside the influence of indigenous languages such as Mapudungun, Quechua, and Guaraní.

gdpDescription.zh: 1840年的阿根廷邦联经济仍以畜牧和初级出口为基础，布宜诺斯艾利斯港口在对外贸易中占绝对优势。经济总量不大，但因牛皮、牛脂、干肉及相关产品出口，在南大西洋与拉普拉塔贸易体系中具有区域重要性。长期内战、封锁和省际分裂抑制了更广泛的市场整合。
gdpDescription.en: In 1840 the economy of the Argentine Confederation remained based on livestock and primary exports, with the port of Buenos Aires dominating external commerce. Total output was modest, but hides, tallow, salted meat, and related products gave it regional importance in the South Atlantic and Río de la Plata trading system. Prolonged civil war, blockades, and provincial fragmentation hindered broader market integration.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、牛皮加工、牛脂提炼、干肉与咸肉生产、制革、港口贸易、运输、手工业、面粉加工、葡萄酒与地方农产
mainIndustries.en: Livestock raising, hide processing, tallow rendering, salted and jerked meat production, tanning, port trade, transport, handicrafts, flour milling, wine, and local agriculture

tradeGoods.zh: 出口以牛皮、牛脂、角骨、咸肉、干肉、羊毛初产品为主；进口则包括纺织品、金属器具、奢侈品、纸张、武器、火药和部分工业制成品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports consisted chiefly of hides, tallow, horn and bone products, salted meat, jerked beef, and early wool products; imports included textiles, metal goods, luxury items, paper, weapons, gunpowder, and various manufactured products.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富分布极不均衡。沿海大地产主以土地、牲畜和贸易网络积累财富；城市中产拥有商铺、房产和存货；广大乡村人口多依赖牲畜、劳动和小块土地，现金持有有限。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Coastal great landowners accumulated wealth through land, herds, and trade networks; urban middling groups held shops, property, and inventories; much of the rural population depended on livestock, labor, and small plots, with limited cash holdings.

economicSystem.zh: 以出口导向的牧场经济、港口海关财政和地方化市场为特征的混合经济。国家对贸易规则和海关有强力干预，但生产本身主要由私人牧场主、商人和省级网络主导。
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy characterized by export-oriented ranching, port-customs finance, and localized markets. The state intervened strongly in trade rules and customs, but production itself was mainly directed by private estancieros, merchants, and provincial networks.

category.zh: 债务、补贴与政治性支付

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库实力在拉普拉塔地区相对可观，主要依赖布宜诺斯艾利斯海关现金流。虽然账面上仍有一定储备，但其稳定性高度依赖港口贸易、外部封锁形势以及各省对罗萨斯领导的服从。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was relatively substantial by Río de la Plata standards and depended mainly on the cash flow of Buenos Aires customs. Although it retained some reserves on paper, its stability depended heavily on port trade, the situation of external blockades, and provincial obedience to Rosas's leadership.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重保守平衡与海关集中，尽量维持军费和秩序支出，同时通过控制进口、优先军政拨款和限制制度化中央分配来确保布宜诺斯艾利斯的主导地位。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized conservative balance and concentration of customs income, seeking to sustain military and order-related spending while preserving the predominance of Buenos Aires through import control, priority military-administrative disbursements, and limited institutionalized central redistribution.

description.zh: 由步兵、骑兵和炮兵组成的常备核心力量，分布于布宜诺斯艾利斯、内战战区和边境据点。
description.en: The permanent core force composed of infantry, cavalry, and artillery, stationed in Buenos Aires, civil-war theaters, and frontier posts.

description.zh: 依托各省考迪罗与乡村征集形成，是机动性很强的骑乘部队，在内战和地方控制中极为重要。
description.en: Raised through provincial caudillos and rural mobilization, these highly mobile mounted forces were crucial in civil war and local control.

description.zh: 负责边境堡垒、防御原住民袭击、保护定居点和维持草原边线。
description.en: Responsible for forts, defense against indigenous raids, protection of settlements, and maintenance of the frontier line.

description.zh: 规模有限，主要用于河口与内河巡逻、运输、护航和支持港口防御，难以与主要海军列强抗衡。
description.en: Limited in size, used mainly for estuary and river patrol, transport, escort, and port defense support, and unable to match major naval powers.

commanderInChief.zh: 胡安·曼努埃尔·德·罗萨斯

technology.zh: 军备处于火绳枪和燧发枪向击发枪过渡前夜，主力仍使用燧发枪、骑兵长枪、军刀和旧式火炮。骑兵机动、地方征发和熟悉地形是优势，但兵工生产、标准化和海军技术较弱。
technology.en: Armament stood on the eve of the transition from flintlock to percussion systems; the main forces still relied on flintlock muskets, cavalry lances, sabers, and older artillery. Cavalry mobility, local recruitment, and terrain familiarity were strengths, but armaments production, standardization, and naval technology were weak.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自国内联邦派与统一派持续内战、乌拉圭与拉普拉塔河口政治冲突、法国封锁压力、与巴西帝国的地缘竞争，以及南部边疆原住民袭击。
threats.en: The main threats came from the continuing Federalist–Unitarian civil wars, political conflict in Uruguay and the Río de la Plata estuary, pressure from the French blockade, geopolitical rivalry with the Brazilian Empire, and indigenous raids along the southern frontier.

populationDescription.zh: 总人口约120万，分布极不均匀，主要集中于布宜诺斯艾利斯、沿海省份和若干内陆城市周边。广阔草原和边疆地区人口稀疏，原住民控制区与国家定居区之间边界仍不稳定。
populationDescription.en: The total population was about 1.2 million and very unevenly distributed, concentrated chiefly in Buenos Aires, the littoral provinces, and the surroundings of several inland cities. Vast pampas and frontier regions remained sparsely populated, and the boundary between indigenous-controlled territories and state-settled areas was still unstable.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为大地产主、港口商人、高级教士和省级强人；中层包括小地主、军官、律师、商贩与手工业者；下层为高乔、佃农、雇工、奴隶和前奴隶、城市贫民及大量边疆人口。原住民社群在国家体制内外同时存在，社会边缘化明显。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of great landowners, port merchants, senior clergy, and provincial strongmen; the middle included small proprietors, officers, lawyers, traders, and artisans; the lower included gauchos, tenants, laborers, slaves and former slaves, the urban poor, and large frontier populations. Indigenous communities existed both inside and outside state structures and were strongly marginalized.

allies.zh: 正式稳定盟友有限，主要依靠与部分阿根廷各省联邦派领袖以及乌拉圭白党势力的政治协同；与英国保持谨慎务实关系。
allies.en: It had few formal stable allies, relying mainly on political coordination with Federalist leaders in the Argentine provinces and with the Blanco faction in Uruguay; relations with Britain were cautious and pragmatic.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量包括流亡和在地的统一派、法国封锁力量、乌拉圭科罗拉多派及与其相连的地区对手；与巴西帝国关系充满猜忌。
enemies.en: Its principal enemies included exiled and domestic Unitarians, the French blockade forces, the Colorado faction in Uruguay and associated regional rivals; relations with the Brazilian Empire were marked by deep suspicion.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策旨在维护布宜诺斯艾利斯对河口航运、海关与邦联代表权的控制，反对外国武力干涉，同时利用省际与周边国家派系斗争巩固罗萨斯的地区主导地位。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy aimed to preserve Buenos Aires control over estuary navigation, customs, and external representation of the Confederation, to resist foreign armed interference, and to use provincial and neighboring factional struggles to strengthen Rosas's regional predominance.

keyInnovations.zh: 本地技术总体仍属前工业与早期工业过渡阶段，关键技术多依赖进口，包括改良港口装卸、印刷出版、金属加工、制革与盐肉加工工艺、河海航运技术和军事火器制造维修。蒸汽技术已为人所知，但在邦联内部应用仍非常有限。
keyInnovations.en: Local technology remained largely in a transitional pre-industrial to early industrial stage, with key techniques mostly imported, including improved port handling, printing, metalworking, leather processing, salted-meat production, river and maritime navigation, and firearms manufacture and repair. Steam technology was known but still very limited in application within the Confederation.

strengths.zh: 拥有拉普拉塔最重要港口与海关收入、强大的牧场出口基础、机动性极高的骑兵力量，以及罗萨斯在联邦派阵营中的个人权威。布宜诺斯艾利斯的财政优势使其在省际政治中具有压倒性筹码。
strengths.en: It possessed the most important port and customs revenues in the Río de la Plata, a strong ranching export base, highly mobile cavalry forces, and Rosas's personal authority within the Federalist camp. The fiscal superiority of Buenos Aires gave it overwhelming leverage in interprovincial politics.

weaknesses.zh: 中央制度薄弱，国家整合不足，对外贸易过度依赖单一港口和初级产品，内战持续消耗资源，政治镇压加深分裂，海军无力抵御欧洲强权封锁，边疆控制也不稳固。
weaknesses.en: Its central institutions were weak, state integration incomplete, foreign trade overly dependent on a single port and primary goods, civil war continued to drain resources, political repression deepened internal divisions, the navy was too weak to resist European blockades, and frontier control remained insecure.

outlook.zh: 短期内政权仍可依靠海关收入、联邦派联盟和强制性秩序维持稳定，但中长期前景取决于能否解决省际宪制问题、缓和对外封锁与地区冲突，并将邦联从个人化统治转变为更持久的国家结构。
outlook.en: In the short term the regime could remain stable through customs revenue, the Federalist coalition, and coercive order, but its medium- to long-term prospects depended on resolving the interprovincial constitutional question, easing blockades and regional conflicts, and transforming the Confederation from personalized rule into a more durable state structure.

description.zh: 以布宜诺斯艾利斯为核心主导外政的邦联体制，内部省份关系复杂。
description.en: A confederated order centered on Buenos Aires, which dominated foreign affairs amid complex provincial relations.

governmentForm.zh: 中央集权的总统制共和国，名义上实行宪政代议制，但政治实际受军人强人、地区精英与危机时期的非常权力深刻影响。
governmentForm.en: A centralized presidential republic, formally constitutional and representative, but in practice heavily shaped by military strongmen, regional elites, and emergency powers during crisis.

socialStructure.zh: 社会结构高度分层，由沿海与高地的地主、军官、教会人士和城市名流居上；其下为商人、手工业者和城市平民；人口主体为安第斯原住民村社农民，另有梅斯蒂索、非洲裔及少量欧洲与亚洲居民。
socialStructure.en: Society was sharply stratified, with coastal and highland landowners, military officers, clergy, and urban notables at the top; below them stood merchants, artisans, and urban commoners; the bulk of the population consisted of Andean indigenous communal peasants, alongside mestizos, Afro-Peruvians, and small European and Asian minorities.

rulingClass.zh: 军政精英、克里奥尔地主寡头、天主教高级神职人员及利马和主要省会的显贵家族。
rulingClass.en: The ruling class consisted of military-political elites, Creole landed oligarchs, senior Catholic clergy, and notable families of Lima and the principal provincial cities.

succession.zh: 总统按宪法程序选举产生，但1840年的政权更替高度依赖军队支持、政变传统与地方势力结盟。
succession.en: The presidency was constitutionally elective, but by 1840 succession in practice depended heavily on army backing, the tradition of coups, and alliances with regional power brokers.

structure.zh: 国家由总统、国务会议、各部、国会与省级行政机关构成。中央政府试图自利马控制全国，但山地交通困难、地方军政首领势力强，使实际统治能力在各省差异很大。
structure.en: The state consisted of the president, the council of state, ministries, congress, and provincial administrative bodies. The central government attempted to rule the country from Lima, but difficult Andean communications and the power of regional military-political bosses made effective control uneven across the provinces.

function.zh: 负责国内行政、治安、地方政府监督、人口登记与政治秩序维持。
function.en: Oversaw internal administration, public order, supervision of local governments, population records, and maintenance of political stability.

function.zh: 负责税收、海关、国库、公共债务、盐烟等财政垄断及政府支付。
function.en: Managed taxation, customs, the treasury, public debt, fiscal monopolies such as salt and tobacco, and government disbursements.

function.zh: 统辖陆军、民兵、沿海防务、军械、征募和有限的海军力量。
function.en: Directed the army, militias, coastal defense, ordnance, recruitment, and the republic’s limited naval forces.

function.zh: 处理与周边共和国及欧洲列强的条约、使节、边界与对外贸易事务。
function.en: Handled treaties, diplomacy, borders, and external trade relations with neighboring republics and European powers.

function.zh: 负责法院系统、法律执行、部分教会财产与国家—教会关系协调。
function.en: Managed the court system, legal administration, aspects of ecclesiastical property, and coordination of state-church relations.

localAdmin.zh: 全国划分为省、县及教区层级行政单位，地方官员由中央任命或受中央影响产生；但许多地区实际受地主、军官和地方显贵支配，中央税收与司法贯彻程度有限。
localAdmin.en: The country was divided into provinces, districts, and parish-level administrative units, with local officials appointed by or strongly influenced by the center; in many areas, however, landlords, military officers, and local notables exercised real authority, and central taxation and justice were only imperfectly enforced.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系继承西班牙殖民法传统并逐步共和化，结合成文法、行政命令、教会法影响及地方习惯。刑事与民事司法制度存在，但执行常受腐败、战争和交通不便掣肘。
legalSystem.en: The legal system inherited the Spanish colonial legal tradition and was gradually republicanized, combining statutory law, executive decrees, ecclesiastical influence, and local custom. Civil and criminal courts existed, but enforcement was often hindered by corruption, warfare, and poor communications.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖海关关税、消费税、国家垄断收入、印花与间接税，另有部分直接征收和地方摊派。独立后旧殖民贡赋名义上削弱，但对原住民和乡村社群的财政负担仍较重。
taxationSystem.en: Fiscal revenue relied chiefly on customs duties, excises, state-monopoly income, stamp taxes, and other indirect levies, along with some direct assessments and local exactions. Colonial tribute had formally been weakened after independence, but indigenous and rural communities still bore heavy fiscal burdens.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为绝对主导宗教，并深刻影响教育、婚姻、节庆与公共生活；同时安第斯地方宗教实践与天主教礼仪长期并存，形成广泛的民间混合信仰。
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was overwhelmingly dominant and deeply shaped education, marriage, festivals, and public life; at the same time, Andean local religious practices coexisted with Catholic ritual, producing widespread popular syncretism.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想受天主教道德观、伊比利亚法理传统、启蒙时代共和主义与军人保守主义共同影响。国家精英强调秩序、主权和共和国合法性，但自由主义与集权主义之间持续冲突。
philosophy.en: Political thought was influenced by Catholic moral ideas, Iberian legal traditions, Enlightenment republicanism, and military conservatism. State elites emphasized order, sovereignty, and republican legitimacy, yet liberal and centralist tendencies remained in constant tension.

writingSystem.zh: 官方书写系统为拉丁字母西班牙文；盖丘亚语、艾马拉语等原住民语言在口头与地方宗教生活中广泛使用，但国家行政和正式教育以西班牙文为主。
writingSystem.en: The official writing system was the Latin alphabet in Spanish; indigenous languages such as Quechua and Aymara were widely used in speech and local religious life, but state administration and formal education were conducted primarily in Spanish.

culturalAchievements.zh: 1840年的秘鲁文化成就在于独立后共和国身份的塑造、印刷出版与政治报刊的发展、利马与库斯科等城市的宗教建筑和殖民遗产延续，以及安第斯音乐、纺织与节庆传统在全国生活中的顽强存在。
culturalAchievements.en: Peru’s cultural achievements in 1840 lay in the shaping of a post-independence republican identity, the growth of printing and political journalism, the continued prominence of religious architecture and colonial heritage in cities such as Lima and Cuzco, and the enduring vitality of Andean music, textile traditions, and festival culture across the country.

languageFamily.zh: 官方主导语言属印欧语系罗曼语族的西班牙语；重要本土语言包括盖丘亚语系与艾马拉语。
languageFamily.en: The dominant official language belonged to the Indo-European Romance family, namely Spanish; major indigenous languages included the Quechuan family and Aymaran.

gdpDescription.zh: 秘鲁经济在1840年仍以农业和矿业为基础，具有明显的殖民时代延续性。沿海和高地之间市场整合不足，内战与联邦瓦解后的军事动员削弱了财政与商业信心。尽管利马与太平洋港口保持外贸联系，但全国总体仍属低度工业化、交通成本高、生产区域分散的经济体。
gdpDescription.en: In 1840 the Peruvian economy remained fundamentally agrarian and mining-based, with strong continuities from the colonial era. Market integration between coast and highlands was limited, and civil conflict and military mobilization after the collapse of the Confederation weakened fiscal and commercial confidence. Although Lima and the Pacific ports maintained foreign trade links, the country as a whole remained a low-industrialization economy marked by high transport costs and dispersed regional production.

mainIndustries.zh: 主要产业包括高地银矿与其他矿业、粮食和畜牧农业、羊驼与羊毛相关生产、甘蔗与酒类加工、纺织手工业、沿海商业和港口服务。1840年瓜诺尚未成为后期那种决定性的财政支柱，但已被逐步注意到其潜力。
mainIndustries.en: Main industries included highland silver mining and other extractive activities, grain and livestock agriculture, alpaca and wool-related production, sugar and spirits processing, artisanal textiles, and coastal commerce and port services. Guano had not yet become the overwhelmingly decisive fiscal pillar it would later be, though its potential was increasingly recognized.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口品以白银、金属、羊毛、硝石小规模产品、糖、皮革、树皮药材及部分农产品为主；进口品包括英国纺织品、金属制成品、工具、武器、奢侈品、纸张与部分工业原料。
tradeGoods.en: Exports consisted mainly of silver, metals, wool, small quantities of nitrates and related products, sugar, hides, medicinal bark, and some agricultural goods; imports included British textiles, metal manufactures, tools, weapons, luxury goods, paper, and certain industrial materials.

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富极不均衡。沿海地主、商人和高阶军政人物可拥有地产、城市房屋、白银储备与商业债权；广大农村家庭财富主要体现为小块土地使用权、牲畜、纺织品和社群互助关系。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Coastal landowners, merchants, and high military-political figures could hold estates, urban houses, silver reserves, and commercial claims; for most rural families, wealth consisted mainly of small cultivation rights, livestock, textiles, and communal support networks.

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度属于以私有财产、庄园经济、矿业特许、手工业生产和国家对海关及若干专卖收入强力干预相结合的早期共和市场经济。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an early republican market economy combining private property, hacienda production, mining concessions, artisanal manufacture, and strong state intervention in customs and selected monopoly revenues.

source.zh: 印花、司法收费与其他杂项
source.en: Stamps, judicial fees, and miscellaneous receipts

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库现金与可动用储备有限，受战争支出、国内政治不稳、税收征解不均和债务压力影响。财政体系尚未获得瓜诺时代那种后来出现的巨额稳定收入，因此流动性紧张时常发生。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury held only limited cash and usable reserves, constrained by wartime spending, domestic instability, uneven tax collection, and debt pressure. The fiscal system had not yet acquired the massive stable revenues later associated with the guano era, so liquidity crises were frequent.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策以维持军队、确保海关收入与短期政权生存为优先，带有强烈应急性。政府倾向于增加进口关税、强化征收和借贷，而非进行系统性的长期财政改革。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining the armed forces, preserving customs revenue, and ensuring short-term regime survival, giving it a strongly improvised character. The government tended to raise import duties, intensify collection, and resort to borrowing rather than undertake deep long-term fiscal reform.

description.zh: 为军队主力，编成线列步兵、轻步兵与守备部队，武器以燧发枪和刺刀为主，训练与补给水平因地区和部队来源差异很大。
description.en: The main arm of the military, organized into line infantry, light infantry, and garrison units, armed chiefly with flintlock muskets and bayonets; standards of training and supply varied greatly by region and unit origin.

description.zh: 用于侦察、快速机动、追击与边境行动，在沿海与部分高原地带较有价值，但受地形和马匹供应限制。
description.en: Used for reconnaissance, rapid movement, pursuit, and frontier operations, it was valuable in coastal zones and parts of the highlands but constrained by terrain and horse supply.

description.zh: 拥有野战炮与要塞炮，数量有限，机动困难，火炮质量和弹药供应不稳定，但在城市防守和决定性会战中仍具重要作用。
description.en: Consisted of field and fortress artillery in limited numbers; mobility was difficult and both gun quality and ammunition supply were uneven, yet artillery remained important in urban defense and decisive engagements.

description.zh: 规模较小，承担港口巡逻、运输、封锁支援和有限海岸防御任务，难以与主要海上列强抗衡，但对太平洋沿岸共和国战争仍有意义。
description.en: Small in scale, it carried out port patrol, transport, blockade support, and limited coastal defense duties; it could not rival major naval powers, but still mattered in wars among Pacific republics.

description.zh: 由地方征集，训练与纪律较差，但在维持省份秩序、补充正规军和支持临时战役方面不可或缺。
description.en: Raised locally, these forces had weaker training and discipline, but were indispensable for provincial order, reinforcement of the regular army, and support during emergency campaigns.

commanderInChief.zh: 总统兼最高统帅阿古斯丁·加马拉

technology.zh: 军事技术处于火药时代后期与工业革命初期之间，主力装备仍为燧发枪、骑兵刀和青铜或铁制滑膛炮；后勤依赖驮畜、征发和港口运输，工业化兵工生产能力薄弱。
technology.en: Military technology stood between the late gunpowder era and the early Industrial Revolution: the main equipment remained flintlock muskets, sabers, and bronze or iron smoothbore cannon; logistics depended on pack animals, requisitioning, and port transport, while domestic industrial arms production was weak.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自与玻利维亚的持续敌意、南部边境动员、国内军阀叛乱、联邦解体后的政治不稳，以及与智利关系中的长期战略猜疑。
threats.en: Principal threats came from continued hostility with Bolivia, mobilization along the southern frontier, internal caudillo rebellions, political instability after the Confederation’s breakup, and enduring strategic mistrust in relations with Chile.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约150万，绝大多数生活在农村，尤其是安第斯高地村社与庄园地带。族群构成以原住民为最多，其次为梅斯蒂索，另有克里奥尔、西班牙裔后裔、非洲裔和少量外国商民。
populationDescription.en: The population was about 1.5 million, with the vast majority living in rural areas, especially in Andean village communities and hacienda zones. Indigenous peoples formed the largest demographic component, followed by mestizos, with Creoles, other people of Spanish descent, Afro-Peruvians, and a small number of foreign merchants and residents also present.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为克里奥尔地主、商人、将领和高级教士；中间层包括地方官员、低级军官、专业人士、工匠和城市商贩；下层则由原住民村社农民、佃农、劳役工、黑人及混血劳动者构成。地方和族群身份在社会等级中与财富同样重要。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of Creole landowners, merchants, generals, and senior clergy; the middle groups included local officials, junior officers, professionals, artisans, and urban petty traders; the lower orders were composed of indigenous communal peasants, tenants, laborers, and Afro-Peruvian and mixed-race workers. Regional and ethnic identity mattered almost as much as wealth in the social hierarchy.

allies.zh: 1840年的秘鲁没有稳定可靠的大国盟友，外交上更依赖临时性的地区安排与同英国、法国等商贸强国维持务实关系。
allies.en: In 1840 Peru had no stable, dependable great-power ally, relying instead on temporary regional arrangements and pragmatic commercial relations with trading powers such as Britain and France.

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是玻利维亚，双方因秘鲁—玻利维亚联邦瓦解后的权力与边界问题持续紧张；同时国内反对派军人集团也是现实威胁。
enemies.en: Its principal enemy was Bolivia, with tensions continuing over power and frontier issues after the breakup of the Peru-Bolivian Confederation; domestic opposition military factions were also a real threat.

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以阻止玻利维亚重新形成对秘鲁南部的战略压力、维护太平洋贸易口岸、争取国际承认与信用、并在智利、厄瓜多尔和新格拉纳达之间保持可操作的地区平衡为核心。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preventing Bolivia from reconstituting strategic pressure on southern Peru, preserving Pacific trade ports, securing international recognition and credit, and maintaining a workable regional balance among Chile, Ecuador, and New Granada.

era.zh: 工业革命早期的安第斯共和国
era.en: An Andean republic in the early Industrial Revolution

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术特征包括改良采矿技术的有限传播、沿海港口商业基础设施、印刷与报刊网络扩大、军用火器和炮兵制度化，以及与英国工业品输入相伴的消费和工具更新；但铁路、电报和大规模机器工业尚未形成全国性转变。
keyInnovations.en: Key technological features included the limited spread of improved mining methods, commercial infrastructure at coastal ports, an expanding printing and newspaper network, the institutionalization of firearms and artillery in warfare, and the updating of tools and consumption patterns through imports of British industrial goods; however, railways, telegraphs, and large-scale machine industry had not yet transformed the country nationally.

strengths.zh: 秘鲁的优势在于拥有太平洋重要港口、利马作为政治与商业中心的持续吸引力、安第斯高地的人口与资源基础、白银和潜在瓜诺财富，以及较强的历史国家传统和文化整合能力。
